ocr_mistral.text:
This Certificate should be authenticated by the Clerk.

# CLERK'S CERTIFICATE.

State of Kentucky County of Henderson SS.

I, James P. Breckenridge, Clerk of the County Court, within and for the County and State aforesaid, do hereby certify that H. S. Park, Esq., before whom the foregoing declaration and affidavit were made, and who has thereunto signed his name, was at the time of so doing a Notary Public in and for the County and State above named, duly commissioned, qualified and sworn; that all his official acts as such are entitled to full faith and credit, and that his said signature as it appears on the reverse page hereof is genuine.

GIVEN under my hand and seal of Said County Court at Office in Henderson, this 21st day of August A. D. 1867

J. F. Breckenridge, Clerk
of Henderson County Court

Remarks.—In case the Deputy administrator the Clerk's certificate, evidence may be required to show that the Deputy is duly appointed and authorized to file in sign the Clerk's name and one the seal of the Court—therefore it is better to have the Clerk's own individual authorizations, at once, and then save the trouble and expense of a general authorization certificate respecting the Deputy, which might be required if the papers were authenticated by him instead of the Clerk.

"Additional Evidence."

APPLICATION FOR

Act of

Case No.

Applicant.

AFFIDAVIT OF

Attorney

PENSION
U APR 21 1887 S
OFFICE

transcribe_handwriting.text:
CLERK'S CERTIFICATE.
State of Kentucky County of Henderson SS.
I, James S. Worthington, Clerk of the Circuit Court, within
and for the County and State aforesaid, do hereby certify that....Fred C. Bush.... Esq., before
whom the foregoing declaration and affidavit were made, and who has theretofore signed his name, was at the time of so
doing a Notary Public in and for the County and State above named, duly commissioned, qualified
and sworn: that all his official acts as such are entitled to full faith and credit, and that his said signature as it appears
on the reverse page hereof is genuine.

GIVEN under my hand and seal of the Circuit Court at Office
in Henderson this 21st day of August A.D. 1897

James S. Rothenheiser Clerk.
of Henderson County  Clerk.

Remarks: It is not the papers authenticates the Clerk or not ..... authenticates be sufficient for purposes [torn] an official...suffer be that before the papers w[unclear] each......be reported if the papers were authenticated by the natural of the Clerk.

AFFIDAVIT OF

"Additional Evidence"
APPLICATION FOR

Attorney.

Act of

Class No.

No.

Applicant.

[Seal: DEPARTMENT OF THE INTERIOR SE PENSION OFFICE]

PENSION DIVISION
